name: rate-policy
description: Contains details about a rate policy.

additionalMatchOptions.description: The list of additional match conditions.

positiveMatch.description: Whether the condition should trigger on a match (`true`) or a lack of match (`false`).

type.description: The match condition type. The `RequestHeaderCondition` listed here is deprecated. Specify `RequestHeaderCondition` as an `atomicCondition` to match on request headers. See [Export match condition type values](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/cndval).

values.description: The list of values that trigger the condition on match.

apiSelectors.description: The API endpoints to match in incoming requests. This only applies to the `api` `matchType`.

definedResources.description: When `true`, match on any resource explicitly added to your API definition without including a `resourceId`. When `false`, you'll need to pass a `resourceId`.

resourceIds.description: The unique identifiers of the endpoint's resources.

undefinedResources.description: When `true`, match on any resource you have not explicitly added to your API definition without including a `resourceId`.  When `false`, you'll need to pass a `resourceId`.

averageThreshold.description: The allowed hits per second during any two-minute interval.

bodyParameters.description: The list of body parameters to match on.

name.description: The name you assign to a body parameter.

positiveMatch.description: Whether the condition should trigger on a match (`true`) or a lack of match (`false`).

valueInRange.description: Whether to match a value inside or outside a range. The range format is `min:max` — for example, `2:4`.

burstThreshold.description: The allowed hits per second during any five-second interval.

burstWindow.description: The time span for the `burstThreshold` interval. For existing rate policies, analyze your traffic in Alert mode before you reduce the measure window from 5 seconds. [Learn more about thresholds here](https://techdocs.akamai.com/kona-site-defender/docs/rate-thresholds).

clientIdentifier.description: The client identifier you want to use to identify and track request senders. The value is required only for WAF type, and `api-key` is supported only for API match criteria. Using `ip-useragent` is typically more specific than using `ip` alone when trying to identify a client. Tracking by `cookie:value` applies to requests per individual session, even if the IP address changes.

condition.description: Contains information about the criteria that trigger the rate policy.

atomicConditions.description: The conditions that trigger the rate policy. Specify one or more request headers, TLS fingerprints, or client reputation categories.

oneOf.description: Collects data needed for condition matches on request headers.

className.description: The type of condition. The `RequestHeaderCondition` type checks for a header from the requesting client and determines if it matches a provided header. This subtype supports the `?` wildcard to match any single character and the `*` wildcard to match any sequence of 0 or more characters. You can also match on case sensitivity.

name.description: A header name. Use `name` to check whether the specified header exists.

nameWildcard.description: Whether to interpret `?` and `*` as wildcards.

positiveMatch.description: Whether the condition triggers on a match or lack of match.

value.description: A list of unique header values. Use both `value` and `name` to check whether the requesting client’s header matches a provided header.

valueCase.description: Whether to consider the case sensitivity of the provided header values.

valueWildcard.description: Whether to interpret `?` and `*` as wildcards.

oneOf.name: RequestHeaderCondition

oneOf.description: Collects data needed for condition matches on TLS fingerprints.

className.description: The type of condition. In this case, `TlsFingerprintCondition`.

positiveMatch.description: Whether the condition triggers on a match or lack of match.

value.description: A list of unique TLS fingerprints.

oneOf.name: TlsFingerprintCondition

oneOf.description: Collects data needed for condition matches on Client Reputation.

className.description: The type of condition. In this case, `ClientReputationCondition`.

name.description: Identifies the reputation category. Web scrapers (`WEBSCRP`) crawl sites and collect data like hotel rates, product prices, store locations, and more. DoS attackers (`DOSATCK`) are web clients or botnets that use automated tools to launch volumetric Denial of Service (DoS) attacks. Web attackers (`WEBATCK`) target websites and web apps with techniques like SQL injection, remote file inclusion, or cross-site scripting. Scanning tools (`SCANTL`) probe web apps for vulnerabilities during an attack's reconnaissance phase.

positiveMatch.description: Whether the condition triggers on a match or lack of match.

value.description: Threshold value that causes the trigger.

oneOf.name: ClientReputationCondition

positiveMatch.description: Whether the condition should trigger on a match (`true`) or a lack of match (`false`).

counterType.description: The rate policy counter type. Either `per_edge` for rate limiting to work per edge node, or `region_aggregated` for rate limiting to work using aggregated rate accounting across multiple edge nodes.

createDate.description: __Read-only__ The time stamp when you created the rate policy.

description.description: Descriptive text you provide about a policy.

evaluation.description: Contains details about rate policy evaluation.

averageThreshold.description: The allowed hits per second during any two-minute interval during evaluation.

burstThreshold.description: The allowed hits per second during any five-second interval during evaluation.

burstWindow.description: __Read-only__ The time span for the `burstThreshold` interval used during evaluation. This value is always set to the `burstWindow` specified by the main rate policy.

counterType.description: The evaluation rate policy counter type. Either `per_edge` for rate limiting to work per edge node, or `region_aggregated` for rate limiting to work using aggregated rate accounting across multiple edge nodes.

endDate.description: __Read-only__ The time stamp when evaluation ends.

startDate.description: __Read-only__ The time stamp when evaluation starts.

fileExtensions.description: Contains the file extension match criteria.

positiveMatch.description: Whether the condition should trigger on a match (`true`) or a lack of match (`false`).

values.description: The file extensions to match on.

hostnames.description: __Deprecated__. The hostnames to match. This array member is deprecated. Use the `hosts` object instead.

hosts.description: The hostnames to match, and whether to trigger on a match or absence of match.

positiveMatch.description: When `true`, triggers on hostnames that match any hostnames in this array. When `false`, triggers on hostnames that don't match any in this array.

values.description: The hostnames you choose to match, or specifically not match.

matchType.description: The match type in a rate policy. Either `path` to match website paths or `api` to match API paths.

name.description: The name you assign to a rate policy.

path.description: Contains details about the path match criteria.

positiveMatch.description: Whether the condition should trigger on a match (`true`) or a lack of match (`false`).

values.description: The list of paths to match on.

pathMatchType.description: The type of paths to match in incoming requests. Either `AllRequests` to match an empty path or any path that ends in a trailing slash (`/`), `TopLevel` to match top-level hostnames only, or `Custom` to match a specific path or path component. This applies only when the corresponding `matchType` member is `path`.

pathUriPositiveMatch.description: Whether the condition should trigger on a match (`true`) or a lack of match (`false`).

queryParameters.description: The list of query parameter objects to match on.

positiveMatch.description: Whether the condition should trigger on a match (`true`) or a lack of match (`false`).

valueInRange.description: Whether to match a value inside or outside a range. The range format is `min:max` — for example, `2:4`.

values.description: The list of query parameter values.

requestType.description: The type of requests to count towards the rate policy's thresholds. Either `ClientRequest` to count client requests to edge servers, `ClientResponse` to count edge responses to the client, `ForwardResponse` to count origin responses to the client, or `ForwardRequest` to count edge requests to your origin.

sameActionOnIpv6.description: Whether to apply the same action to the IPv6 traffic as to the IPv4 traffic.

type.description: The rate policy type. Either `WAF` for Web Application Firewall, or `BOTMAN` for Bot Manager.

updateDate.description: __Read-only__ The ISO 8601 timestamp when you last updated the rate policy.

useXForwardForHeaders.description: Whether to check the contents of the `X-Forwarded-For` header in incoming requests.

used.description: __Read-only__ Whether you're currently using the rate policy.
